title: Race-Lap Pace Residual State
summary: Recent literature models tyre degradation as a latent state with pit-stop resets, and lap time should be separated from fuel burn, track evolution, and driver baseline.
rationale: Recent literature models tyre degradation as a latent state with pit-stop resets, and lap time should be separated from fuel burn, track evolution, and driver baseline. The tried rolling pace features were simple; a normalized residual state may be cleaner.
implementation_hint: Within each Year/Race/LapNumber compute field median lap time and robust z-score. Within Driver/Race/Stint compute EWMA residual trend, residual acceleration, residual versus first stint laps, and cumulative positive residual since stint start. Shift historical rolling features by one row within driver-race-stint where possible.
expected_effect: Can capture degradation cliffs and abnormal performance loss while controlling for lap-wide conditions that affect every driver.
risk: Rows must be sorted and restored carefully; using current-lap field aggregates is acceptable only if those features exist for all test rows, but target-derived future information must be avoided.
